title: Jacea hyrcanica
intro: Jacea hyrcanica, also known as the Hyrcanian knapweed or Jacea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Jacea hyrcanica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. Jacea hyrcanica is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to make cough syrups and expectorants.
taxonomy: Jacea hyrcanica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Jacea, which is a group of plants commonly known as meadow rue. Jacea hyrcanica is native to the region of Hyrcania, now part of modern-day Iran. This plant is further classified into the order Rosales and the class Magnoliopsida. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the current taxonomy.
